title: Market Revolution: industrialization
description: Test your skills on the causes and effects on the innovations in technology, agriculture, and commerce over time.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The rapid deterioration of wooden machinery created powerful incentives to fashion equipment out of brass, iron, and steel, which required more precise fabrication of hard metal parts. Improved grinding and milling machines, drills, metal planes, and machine tools of all types poured forth from workshops in France, Great Britain, and America. These innovations allowed toolmakers to perfect rough castings and fit ever-faster speeds. Everything from clocks and watches to farm machinery and railway locomotives benefited over and over again from the constant improvement of the tools for making machines.”

>-Source: John Lauritz Larson, “The Market Revolution in America, Liberty, Ambition, and the Eclipse of the Common Good,” 2010

**The developments described in the excerpt best illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Improvements in the manufacturing of goods
Decline in the purchase of domestic products
Increase in government funding for internal improvements
Establishment of a new foreign trade relationship with France

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The rapid deterioration of wooden machinery created powerful incentives to fashion equipment out of brass, iron, and steel, which required more precise fabrication of hard metal parts. Improved grinding and milling machines, drills, metal planes, and machine tools of all types poured forth from workshops in France, Great Britain, and America. These innovations allowed toolmakers to perfect rough castings and fit ever-faster speeds. Everything from clocks and watches to farm machinery and railway locomotives benefited over and over again from the constant improvement of the tools for making machines.”

>-Source: John Lauritz Larson, “The Market Revolution in America, Liberty, Ambition, and the Eclipse of the Common Good,” 2010

**Which of the following most directly contributed to the developments described in the excerpt?**	

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The emergence of interchangeable parts and improvements to manufactured technology
The addition of new territory after Jefferson purchased the Louisiana Territory from France
The spread of canals and railroads as a byproduct of Henry Clay’s American System
The growth of factories that practiced the “Lowell System” as a means of maintaining efficiency

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The rapid deterioration of wooden machinery created powerful incentives to fashion equipment out of brass, iron, and steel, which required more precise fabrication of hard metal parts. Improved grinding and milling machines, drills, metal planes, and machine tools of all types poured forth from workshops in France, Great Britain, and America. These innovations allowed toolmakers to perfect rough castings and fit ever-faster speeds. Everything from clocks and watches to farm machinery and railway locomotives benefited over and over again from the constant improvement of the tools for making machines.”

>-Source: John Lauritz Larson, “The Market Revolution in America, Liberty, Ambition, and the Eclipse of the Common Good,” 2010

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that technological developments had on American society?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It was cheaper and easier to develop manufactured products.
It made it more difficult to sell goods to foreign markets.
It made artisanal goods more profitable and sought after.
It led to the decline of women in the workforce.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7196b0187397ff54ccf34b56eba3a93f.png)

>-Source: [US Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**The overall trend from 1800 to 1840 depicted on the graph resulted from which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: new agricultural inventions like the Cotton Gin
new cotton state unions like the Grange
new economic policies stemming from a laissez-faire philosophy
new tariffs that supported American-produced goods over foreign goods

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7196b0187397ff54ccf34b56eba3a93f.png)

>-Source: [US Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following developments most directly relates to the overall trend from 1800 to 1840 depicted on the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: agricultural inventions increased the efficiency of production methods
infrastructure improvements that made traveling faster
tariffs that increased the cost of goods produced in the United States
economic policies that granted subsidies to cotton farmers

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7196b0187397ff54ccf34b56eba3a93f.png)

>-Source: [US Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1800 to 1840 as shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The development of the Cotton Gin
The reemergence of the  Atlantic Slave Trade
The creation of barbed wire
The increase in the supply of cotton

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1bafd9cb03ccef94988a3cb41cf40cd4.png)

>-Source: User: Citynoise, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_road_map.png)

**Which of the following most directly contributed to the creation of the Cumberland Road as depicted on the map?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: new federal funding that supported the development of infrastructure projects
new innovations in the production of steel like the Bessemer process
new agricultural inventions like the Cotton Gin and the Reaper
new efforts by the Spanish to claim ownership of the Mississippi and Ohio Rivers

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1bafd9cb03ccef94988a3cb41cf40cd4.png)

>-Source: User: Citynoise, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_road_map.png)

**A significant long-term result of the major pattern depicted on the map was which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The North and West developed a strong relationship because they were linked through transportation networks.
The South sought to industrialize to keep up with the North and West as they continued to modernize.
The federal government began limiting westward expansion to reduce conflicts with indigenous nations.
The federal government offered citizenship to immigrants who were willing to industrialize the West.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4d37045ebdf2d5cc04f8bde4ea584462.png)

>-Source: William L. Sheppard, “The First Cotton-Gin,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:William_L._Sheppard_-_First_use_of_the_Cotton_Gin,_Harper%27s_weekly,_18_Dec._1869,_p._813.png) 1869

**Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The emergence of new agriculture technologies
The introduction of the Atlantic Slave Trade
The creation of farming organizations like the Grange
The development of the American System

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4d37045ebdf2d5cc04f8bde4ea584462.png)

>-Source: William L. Sheppard, “The First Cotton-Gin,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:William_L._Sheppard_-_First_use_of_the_Cotton_Gin,_Harper%27s_weekly,_18_Dec._1869,_p._813.png) 1869

**The activity shown in the image contributed most directly to which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the number of Northern textile factories
A decrease in international trade
An increase in the price of Southern cotton
A decline in the need for enslaved labor

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4d37045ebdf2d5cc04f8bde4ea584462.png)

>-Source: William L. Sheppard, “The First Cotton-Gin,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:William_L._Sheppard_-_First_use_of_the_Cotton_Gin,_Harper%27s_weekly,_18_Dec._1869,_p._813.png) 1869

**Which of the following developments is the most direct effect of the situation portrayed in the image? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increase expansion to international markets
the end of the Atlantic Slave Trade
the passage of the Missouri Compromise of 1820
the change towards an isolationist foreign policy
